ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: ISO 42001 AI management system and continual improvement; NIST Govern
Implementation notes: Use managed settings for non-negotiable controls such as allowManagedHooksOnly and managed permission rules.

Primary control: Deterministic deny rules for .env, secrets/, SSH, cloud creds, system credential paths
Main risk addressed: Sensitive information disclosure; prompt injection leading to secret exposure
ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: NIST Map data access and Manage risk treatment
Implementation notes: Prefer explicit deny rules over advisory ignore patterns. Use deny, ask, allow with source visibility in /permissions.
Allow structural visibility where needed, but never expose live values. Keep redaction as a usability layer, not the only security control.

Primary control: Deny rules for curl, wget, nc, sudo, su, rm -rf, git push --force
ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: NIST Manage with least privilege and constrained actions
Implementation notes: Block known-bad commands without prompting. Reserve prompts for bounded medium-risk commands only.

Primary control: Managed PreToolUse Bash hook inspecting full command strings
ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: ISO 42001 control effectiveness; NIST Measure and Manage
Implementation notes: Use hooks to inspect full Bash payloads for chaining, decode-and-execute, and mixed read-plus-egress patterns.

Primary control: Sandbox domain allowlist; deny generic download/exfiltration tools
Main risk addressed: Secret exfiltration; supply chain and prompt-driven misuse
ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: NIST Map external dependencies and Manage exposure
Implementation notes: Use sandbox network controls and approved domains such as GitHub and package registries instead of broad shell-based network access.

Primary control: Sandboxed allow-read / allow-write plus deny-read / deny-write paths
Main risk addressed: Access outside intended workspace; local secret discovery
ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: NIST Map system context and Manage boundaries
Implementation notes: Sandboxing provides OS-layer filesystem isolation and reduces prompt frequency while preserving explicit deny behavior.

Primary control: Deny writes to .git/ and .husky/; deny force push; ask for higher-impact GitHub actions
ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: ISO 42001 accountability and change control; NIST Govern
Implementation notes: Allow low-risk read operations, prompt for consequential actions, and block irreversible or control-bypassing actions.

Primary control: Use allowlists for safe repetitive actions plus sandbox auto-allow within boundaries
Main risk addressed: Approval fatigue and unsafe click-through behavior
ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: ISO 42001 continual improvement; NIST Measure and Manage
Implementation notes: Tune ask-to-allow over time using telemetry while keeping hard denies stable.

ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: ISO 42001 governance and awareness; NIST Govern
Implementation notes: CLAUDE.md gives behavioral guidance; hooks and settings provide deterministic enforcement.

Primary control: /permissions, hook logs, approval/denial trends, exception review
Main risk addressed: Control drift; excessive prompts; weak exceptions
Implementation notes: Review recurring prompts, denied commands, and exception requests to refine controls without weakening the hard boundary.

Primary control: Managed hooks; deny rules for shell piping to interpreters; CLAUDE.md behavioural constraints
Main risk addressed: Prompt injection leading to arbitrary code execution
ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: NIST Govern and Manage; ISO 42001 risk treatment
Implementation notes: Hooks inspect payloads before execution. Deny rules block pipe-to-shell patterns. CLAUDE.md instructs the agent to treat untrusted repo content with caution.
Threat vector: Malicious README or CLAUDE.md files in untrusted repos
Claude Code manifestation: Agent executes hidden payloads during build or refactor
Organizational impact: Critical: Arbitrary code execution and system compromise

Primary control: Sandbox filesystem and network boundaries; deny destructive commands
Main risk addressed: Unsanitized generated commands causing data loss
ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: NIST Manage with least privilege; ISO 42001 control effectiveness
Implementation notes: Sandbox prevents generated commands from reaching production systems. Deny rules block destructive patterns regardless of how they were generated.
Threat vector: Unsanitized shell commands or SQL queries generated by the agent
Claude Code manifestation: Agent deletes production tables or exposes sensitive system paths
Organizational impact: High: Data loss and unauthorized system modification

Primary control: Deny rules for credential paths; sandbox denyRead paths
ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: NIST Map data access and Manage exposure; ISO 42001 risk treatment
Implementation notes: Defence in depth: deny rules block access, redaction hooks strip values from permitted structural reads, sandbox enforces OS-level file isolation.
Threat vector: Ingestion of .env .ssh or .kube config files into context
Claude Code manifestation: Persistent leakage of credentials across session API calls
Organizational impact: Critical: Long-term credential theft and lateral movement

Primary control: Managed permission tiers (deny/ask/allow); sandbox with no unsandboxed commands; managed-only MCP servers
Main risk addressed: Unrestricted agent access leading to exfiltration
ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: NIST Govern and Manage; ISO 42001 AI management system
Implementation notes: Layered permissions ensure the agent cannot act beyond its approved scope. Network egress is limited to allowlisted domains. MCP servers are locked to a managed list.
Threat vector: Granting unrestricted Bash or filesystem access to the agent
Claude Code manifestation: Agent performs network exfiltration via curl or wget
Organizational impact: High: Data exfiltration and resource abuse

Primary control: Managed-only hooks and MCP servers; sandbox resource boundaries; ask rules for install and build commands
Main risk addressed: Over-permissive tooling enabling recursive or mass operations
ISO 42001 / NIST AI RMF alignment: ISO 42001 continual improvement; NIST Measure and Manage
Implementation notes: Restrict tool availability to managed definitions. Use ask rules for resource-intensive operations. Monitor for unusual patterns in hook and approval logs.
Threat vector: Exploiting over-permissive tool rules for recursive operations
Claude Code manifestation: Resource exhaustion or unintended mass modification of repositories
Organizational impact: Moderate: Operational disruption and Denial of Wallet
